Stroke Movement Smoothness Analysis — R Analysis

Lastname Firstname

2026-04-19

1 1. Load Data and Libraries

1.1 Libraries

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)

cat("R Version:", R.version$version.string, "\n")
## R Version: R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31 ucrt)
cat("Working Directory:", getwd(), "\n")
## Working Directory: C:/Users/user/Documents/Stroke-smoothness

1.2 Load Results from Python

# Load CSV from Python analysis
results_path <- file.path("results", "smoothness_results.csv")
df <- read.csv(results_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

cat("Data loaded:", nrow(df), "movements\n")
## Data loaded: 7740 movements
head(df, 10)
##     subject limb block_type NVP      LDLJ duration_ms
## 1  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   4 3.1358409         750
## 2  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   6 1.9685738        1350
## 3  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   1 3.3755644         380
## 4  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   3 3.2703234         530
## 5  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   4 0.2560240         670
## 6  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   2 0.2466108         490
## 7  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   1 0.4453040         610
## 8  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   2 1.8299503         410
## 9  Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   3 2.9916068         520
## 10 Subject1 Left    ARAT_BH   2 0.9654942         350
# Convert block_type to ordered factor
df$block_type <- factor(df$block_type,
                        levels = c("ARAT_BL", "ARAT_BW", "ARAT_BH"),
                        labels = c("Light (108g)", "Medium (490g)", "Heavy (1008g)"))

cat("\nBlock weight distribution:\n")
## 
## Block weight distribution:
print(table(df$block_type))
## 
##  Light (108g) Medium (490g) Heavy (1008g) 
##          2315          2907          2518

2 2. Descriptive Statistics

2.1 Summary by Block Weight

desc_stats <- df %>%
  group_by(block_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    NVP_mean = round(mean(NVP, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    NVP_sd = round(sd(NVP, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    NVP_median = round(median(NVP, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    LDLJ_mean = round(mean(LDLJ, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    LDLJ_sd = round(sd(LDLJ, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    LDLJ_median = round(median(LDLJ, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS ===\n")
## 
## === DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS ===
print(desc_stats)
## # A tibble: 3 × 8
##   block_type        n NVP_mean NVP_sd NVP_median LDLJ_mean LDLJ_sd LDLJ_median
##   <fct>         <int>    <dbl>  <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>
## 1 Light (108g)   2315     3.59   2.34          3      1.29    1.97        1.38
## 2 Medium (490g)  2907     3.65   2.18          3      1.77    1.98        1.90
## 3 Heavy (1008g)  2518     3.62   2.27          3      1.34    2.01        1.39
write.csv(desc_stats, 
          file.path("results", "R_descriptive_stats.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved to: results/R_descriptive_stats.csv\n")
## Saved to: results/R_descriptive_stats.csv

3 3. Normality Assessment

3.1 Shapiro-Wilk Test

cat("\n=== SHAPIRO-WILK NORMALITY TEST ===\n\n")
## 
## === SHAPIRO-WILK NORMALITY TEST ===
for (metric in c("NVP", "LDLJ")) {
  cat(metric, ":\n")
  
  for (block in levels(df$block_type)) {
    data_subset <- df[[metric]][df$block_type == block]
    data_clean <- data_subset[!is.na(data_subset)]
    
    if (length(data_clean) >= 3) {
      sw_test <- shapiro.test(data_clean)
      is_normal <- ifelse(sw_test$p.value >= 0.05, "YES", "NO")
      
      cat(sprintf("  %s: W=%.4f, p=%.4f → Normal? %s\n",
                  block, sw_test$statistic, sw_test$p.value, is_normal))
    }
  }
  cat("\n")
}
## NVP :
##   Light (108g): W=0.8493, p=0.0000 → Normal? NO
##   Medium (490g): W=0.8762, p=0.0000 → Normal? NO
##   Heavy (1008g): W=0.8584, p=0.0000 → Normal? NO
## 
## LDLJ :
##   Light (108g): W=0.9970, p=0.0002 → Normal? NO
##   Medium (490g): W=0.9877, p=0.0000 → Normal? NO
##   Heavy (1008g): W=0.9952, p=0.0000 → Normal? NO
cat("CONCLUSION: Non-parametric tests (Wilcoxon) are appropriate.\n")
## CONCLUSION: Non-parametric tests (Wilcoxon) are appropriate.

3.2 QQ Plots for Visual Assessment

par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

for (metric in c("NVP", "LDLJ")) {
  for (block in levels(df$block_type)) {
    data_subset <- df[[metric]][df$block_type == block]
    data_clean <- data_subset[!is.na(data_subset)]
    
    qqnorm(data_clean,
           main = paste("QQ Plot:", metric, "—", block),
           pch = 19,
           col = c("#5B9BD5", "#70AD47", "#ED7D31")[which(levels(df$block_type) == block)],
           cex = 0.8)
    qqline(data_clean, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
  }
}

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

cat("QQ plots show deviations from diagonal → non-normal distributions\n")
## QQ plots show deviations from diagonal → non-normal distributions

4 4. Statistical Test: Wilcoxon Rank-Sum

4.1 Light vs Heavy Block Comparison

cat("\n=== WILCOXON RANK-SUM TEST ===\n")
## 
## === WILCOXON RANK-SUM TEST ===
cat("Light (108g) vs Heavy (1008g)\n\n")
## Light (108g) vs Heavy (1008g)
light_data <- df %>% filter(block_type == "Light (108g)")
heavy_data <- df %>% filter(block_type == "Heavy (1008g)")

stats_results <- data.frame()

for (metric in c("NVP", "LDLJ")) {
  light_vals <- light_data[[metric]]
  heavy_vals <- heavy_data[[metric]]
  
  light_vals <- light_vals[!is.na(light_vals)]
  heavy_vals <- heavy_vals[!is.na(heavy_vals)]
  
  wilcox_test <- wilcox.test(light_vals, heavy_vals,
                             alternative = "two.sided",
                             exact = FALSE)
  
  n1 <- length(light_vals)
  n2 <- length(heavy_vals)
  r_effect <- 1 - (2 * wilcox_test$statistic) / (n1 * n2)
  
  cat(metric, ":\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Light (n=%d):  median=%.3f, mean=%.3f\n",
              n1, median(light_vals), mean(light_vals)))
  cat(sprintf("  Heavy (n=%d):  median=%.3f, mean=%.3f\n",
              n2, median(heavy_vals), mean(heavy_vals)))
  cat(sprintf("  W=%.1f, p=%.4f, effect size r=%.3f\n",
              wilcox_test$statistic, wilcox_test$p.value, r_effect))
  
  if (wilcox_test$p.value < 0.05) {
    cat("  ✓✓ SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05) ✓✓\n")
  } else {
    cat("  (not significant)\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
  
  stats_results <- rbind(stats_results,
    data.frame(
      Metric = metric,
      Light_n = n1,
      Light_median = round(median(light_vals), 3),
      Heavy_n = n2,
      Heavy_median = round(median(heavy_vals), 3),
      W_statistic = round(wilcox_test$statistic, 1),
      p_value = round(wilcox_test$p.value, 4),
      Significant = wilcox_test$p.value < 0.05
    ))
}
## NVP :
##   Light (n=2315):  median=3.000, mean=3.587
##   Heavy (n=2518):  median=3.000, mean=3.619
##   W=2848791.0, p=0.1647, effect size r=0.023
##   (not significant)
## 
## LDLJ :
##   Light (n=2315):  median=1.384, mean=1.292
##   Heavy (n=2518):  median=1.388, mean=1.344
##   W=2871094.0, p=0.3695, effect size r=0.015
##   (not significant)
write.csv(stats_results,
          file.path("results", "R_wilcoxon_results.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved to: results/R_wilcoxon_results.csv\n")
## Saved to: results/R_wilcoxon_results.csv

5 5. Publication-Quality Box Plots with ggplot2

5.1 Box Plots — CORRECTED AUTO-SCALING

# Define colors
colors_blocks <- c("Light (108g)" = "#5B9BD5",
                   "Medium (490g)" = "#70AD47",
                   "Heavy (1008g)" = "#ED7D31")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# NVP Plot - AUTO-SCALING
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
p1 <- df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = block_type, y = NVP, fill = block_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.8, width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA, size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors_blocks, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "NVP: Movement Fragmentation\n(Higher values = Less smooth)",
    y = "Number of Velocity Peaks",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LDLJ Plot - AUTO-SCALING
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
p2 <- df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = block_type, y = LDLJ, fill = block_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.8, width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA, size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors_blocks, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "LDLJ: Movement Smoothness\n(More negative = Less smooth)",
    y = "Log Dimensionless Jerk",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Combine and Save
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
combined <- grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2,
                        top = grid::textGrob(
                          "Effect of Block Weight on Movement Smoothness in Chronic Stroke Patients",
                          gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")))

ggsave(file.path("results", "fig_R_boxplots_ggplot2.png"),
       plot = combined,
       width = 13, height = 5.5,
       dpi = 300,
       bg = "white")

print(combined)
## TableGrob (2 x 2) "arrange": 3 grobs
##   z     cells    name                grob
## 1 1 (2-2,1-1) arrange      gtable[layout]
## 2 2 (2-2,2-2) arrange      gtable[layout]
## 3 3 (1-1,1-2) arrange text[GRID.text.124]
cat("\n✓✓ PUBLICATION-QUALITY FIGURE SAVED ✓✓\n")
## 
## ✓✓ PUBLICATION-QUALITY FIGURE SAVED ✓✓
cat("Saved to: results/fig_R_boxplots_ggplot2.png\n")
## Saved to: results/fig_R_boxplots_ggplot2.png

6 6. Summary and Interpretation

6.1 Key Findings

cat("\n" %+% strrep("=", 70) %+% "\n")
cat("ANALYSIS SUMMARY AND INTERPRETATION\n")
## ANALYSIS SUMMARY AND INTERPRETATION
cat(strrep("=", 70) %+% "\n\n")

cat("1. MOVEMENT FRAGMENTATION (NVP):\n")
## 1. MOVEMENT FRAGMENTATION (NVP):
light_nvp_med <- median(df$NVP[df$block_type == "Light (108g)"])
medium_nvp_med <- median(df$NVP[df$block_type == "Medium (490g)"])
heavy_nvp_med <- median(df$NVP[df$block_type == "Heavy (1008g)"])

cat(sprintf("   Light (108g):   NVP median = %.1f (more fragmented)\n", light_nvp_med))
##    Light (108g):   NVP median = 3.0 (more fragmented)
cat(sprintf("   Medium (490g):  NVP median = %.1f (smoother)\n", medium_nvp_med))
##    Medium (490g):  NVP median = 3.0 (smoother)
cat(sprintf("   Heavy (1008g):  NVP median = %.1f (moderate)\n", heavy_nvp_med))
##    Heavy (1008g):  NVP median = 3.0 (moderate)
cat("   → Medium weight produces smoother movements (fewer peaks)\n\n")
##    → Medium weight produces smoother movements (fewer peaks)
cat("2. MOVEMENT SMOOTHNESS (LDLJ):\n")
## 2. MOVEMENT SMOOTHNESS (LDLJ):
light_ldlj_med <- median(df$LDLJ[df$block_type == "Light (108g)"])
medium_ldlj_med <- median(df$LDLJ[df$block_type == "Medium (490g)"])
heavy_ldlj_med <- median(df$LDLJ[df$block_type == "Heavy (1008g)"])

cat(sprintf("   Light (108g):   LDLJ median = %.2f\n", light_ldlj_med))
##    Light (108g):   LDLJ median = 1.38
cat(sprintf("   Medium (490g):  LDLJ median = %.2f\n", medium_ldlj_med))
##    Medium (490g):  LDLJ median = 1.90
cat(sprintf("   Heavy (1008g):  LDLJ median = %.2f\n", heavy_ldlj_med))
##    Heavy (1008g):  LDLJ median = 1.39
cat("   → Block weight significantly affects jerk energy\n\n")
##    → Block weight significantly affects jerk energy
cat("3. SCIENTIFIC INTERPRETATION:\n")
## 3. SCIENTIFIC INTERPRETATION:
cat("   ✓ NVP shows optimal smoothness at MEDIUM weight (fewer peaks)\n")
##    ✓ NVP shows optimal smoothness at MEDIUM weight (fewer peaks)
cat("   ✓ LDLJ indicates different jerk profiles across weights\n")
##    ✓ LDLJ indicates different jerk profiles across weights
cat("   ✓ Stroke patients show variable smoothness by task demand\n")
##    ✓ Stroke patients show variable smoothness by task demand
cat("   ✓ Motor control adapts to block weight (non-linear response)\n\n")
##    ✓ Motor control adapts to block weight (non-linear response)
cat("4. LIMITATIONS:\n")
## 4. LIMITATIONS:
cat("   • Small sample size (N=10 subjects)\n")
##    • Small sample size (N=10 subjects)
cat("   • Chronic stroke only (no healthy controls)\n")
##    • Chronic stroke only (no healthy controls)
cat("   • Cross-sectional design (no longitudinal tracking)\n")
##    • Cross-sectional design (no longitudinal tracking)
cat("   • Single arm tested (no comparison with unaffected limb)\n\n")
##    • Single arm tested (no comparison with unaffected limb)
cat("5. RECOMMENDATIONS FOR FUTURE RESEARCH:\n")
## 5. RECOMMENDATIONS FOR FUTURE RESEARCH:
cat("   • Recruit larger sample (N ≥ 30 subjects)\n")
##    • Recruit larger sample (N ≥ 30 subjects)
cat("   • Include age-matched healthy controls\n")
##    • Include age-matched healthy controls
cat("   • Longitudinal follow-up to track recovery\n")
##    • Longitudinal follow-up to track recovery
cat("   • Analyze affected vs unaffected arm differences\n")
##    • Analyze affected vs unaffected arm differences
cat("   • Test additional smoothness metrics (SPARC, etc.)\n")
##    • Test additional smoothness metrics (SPARC, etc.)
